text: We would like to present a case of a robotic duodenal jejunostomy being performed in a patient with SMA syndrome. A twenty-three-year-old female with a past medical history significant for five years of episodic crampy abdominal pain and bloating presented to us with abdominal pain and nausea. CT scan shows a markedly distended stomach and proximal duodenum with a transition point at the level of the superior mesenteric artery. The aorta mesenteric angle measures approximately thirteen degrees when viewed on sagittal imaging. A multidisciplinary approach with gastroenterology was undertaken. and the decision was made to proceed to the operating room for elective bypass of the SMA with a robotic duodenal jejunostomy.

The surgeon traces the jejunum proximally to the ligament of Treitz using DaVinci Firefly fluorescence imaging to illuminate the colonic vasculature. A vessel sealer enters the transverse mesocolon under fluoroscopic guidance. The third portion of the duodenum is identified and partially Kocherized. A tension-free segment of the proximal jejunum is positioned over the third portion of the duodenum for anastomosis, with a 3-0 V-Loc suture used to create the back wall of the anastomosis.

The surgeon uses an XI permanent pottery hook to create incisions for the jejunotomy and duodenotomy on the anti-mesenteric wall of the small bowel. A SureForm 60mm XI reloadable stapler is then used to secure the duodenal jejunal anastomosis, with the thick arm placed in the jejunotomy and the thin arm in the duodenotomy. After ensuring no redundancy in the small bowel tissue, the stapler is clamped and fired. The duodenal jejunostomy is closed, and the stapled anastomosis is over-sewn with 3-0 V-lock sutures. The mesenteric defect is closed with a 2-0 V-lock suture to prevent future herniation.

The surgeon sprays five milliliters of Tis-Seal fibrin sealant onto the anastomosis to secure it. The anastomosis is then irrigated with normal saline, and an endoscopic leak test is performed, showing no bubbles, indicating a negative leak test.
